race.name: Niseko Classic
race.tagline: Asia's premier mountain gran fondo qualifier in Hokkaido's legendary ski terrain

climate.description: Early summer in Hokkaido's mountainous Niseko region; mild temperatures with potential rain in forested and alpine terrain

terrain.surface: Mountainous gran fondo routes through Hokkaido's Niseko range, featuring alpine climbs, forested descents, and coastal views of the Sea of Japan
features: Mountainous gran fondo routes through Hokkaido's Niseko range, featuring alpine climbs, forested descents, and coastal views of the Sea of Japan

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 74. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 70.

course_description.character: A dramatic mountain gran fondo through Hokkaido's famous Niseko ski region, combining steep alpine climbs with fast descents, forested passages, and glimpses of the Sea of Japan. The 140 km route is a true mountain stage with over 2,370 meters of elevation gain, featuring a signature 20 km climb to 1,000 meters elevation.
course_description.signature_challenge: The Niseko Panorama Line climb to Hokkaido's highest KOM, followed by a fast descent into Kyowa Town
suffering_zones: 20 km climb to 1,000 m elevation on the Niseko Panorama Line (home to Hokkaido's highest KOM)
Multiple rolling climbs through forested terrain in the Niseko Mountain Range
Sustained elevation gain throughout the route with limited flat recovery sections

lodging_strategy.name: Niseko Landmark View
lodging_strategy.features: Secure bicycle storage, maintenance area in basement, air pumps for rent, bike-in/bike-out access, fully equipped kitchen, 90+ sq meters of space
lodging_strategy.room_types: Deluxe (1st-3rd floors) and Premium (4th-6th floors) with forest or Mt. Yotei views

lodging_strategy.name: Official Host Hotels
lodging_strategy.notes: Multiple options available in Niseko area

logistics.transport: Niseko is located in Hokkaido, Japan, approximately 100 km southwest of Sapporo city and 2 hours driving distance from Sapporo New Chitose Airport (CTS). The event starts and finishes at Niseko Tokyu Grand Hirafu venue. Accommodation options include Niseko Landmark View (bike-in/bike-out access with secure storage and maintenance area) and official host hotels offering 10% discounts.

history.origin_story: Niseko Classic was founded in 2014 as a UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier on a difficult and scenic mountain route in Hokkaido's famous ski region. The event has grown to become Asia's premier amateur cycling event and is now the official pre-event for the 2026 UCI Gran Fondo World Championships—the first time this championship is hosted in Asia.
notable_moments: 2014: Event founded as UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier
2025: 12th edition and 10th year as official UCI-sanctioned citizen race
2025: Introduction of Japan's first Team Relay format (June 13)
2025: First fully closed roads in recent editions
2026: Hosting UCI Gran Fondo World Championships (August 26-30)—Asia's first championship edition
2026: Courses run in opposite direction to past years to prepare for World Championships

biased_opinion.summary: Niseko Classic delivers world-class mountain cycling in an exotic, underexplored region. The elevation and technical climbing rival European gran fondos, while the Hokkaido setting offers cultural immersion and stunning scenery. As the official 2026 UCI Gran Fondo World Championships qualifier and Asia's premier amateur cycling event, it carries significant prestige. However, high travel costs, uncertain registration fees, and limited field depth information temper the overall value proposition for international riders.

final_verdict.one_liner: Niseko Classic is Asia's most prestigious gran fondo, offering world-class mountain cycling and cultural immersion as the official qualifier for the 2026 UCI Gran Fondo World Championships.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Niseko Classic stands as a landmark event in amateur cycling, combining exceptional elevation gain (2,370 m), technical mountain terrain, and stunning Hokkaido scenery. Its status as the official 2026 UCI Gran Fondo World Championships qualifier elevates its prestige significantly. The event delivers on climbing quality and organization, with fully closed roads and professional support. However, international participants face substantial travel costs, uncertain registration fees, and limited information on field depth. Best suited for experienced climbers seeking a unique Asian cycling experience and potential qualification pathway to the World Championships. The June timing in early summer Hokkaido offers mild conditions but potential rain. Overall, a high-quality event with significant cultural and competitive value, though not optimized for budget-conscious riders.

videos.title: Niseko Classic 150km 2022
videos.description: The Niseko classic in Niseko, Japan is back after a three year hiatus. Its a 94 mile course with 8500 ft of elevation. It is a UCI Gran Fondo world series qualifier and is very competitive. It is a great ride through the countryside.
videos.curation_reason: Comprehensive 5+ hour first-person ride-along of the full 150km course with detailed race experience.

videos.description:
#ロードレース #自転車 #北海道 #ニセコ 
【140㎞ロードレース　80㎞ロードレース　本格的な山岳グランフォンドレース】

2025年に第12回を迎える、「ニセコクラシックレース」が、UCI（国際自転車競技連合）の傘下にある公認市民レース、UCIグランフォンドワールドシリーズ（＝UCI Gran Fondo World Series、以下UGFWS）は10年目に突入。 北海道・ニセコ地区を世界へ発信いたします。
